    Index

    A C H L T 
    All Classes and Interfaces|All Packages

    A

    ASTParser - Class in Unnamed Package
    Represents a Java program in an Abstract Syntax Tree (AST), utilizing JavaParser's parsing functionality, converting all parts of a Java program to custom objects storing individual classes, methods, statements, expressions, etc.
    ASTParser(String) - Constructor for class ASTParser
    Parses given Java file and reads in all fields, methods, and constructors

    C

    changeField(String, String) - Method in class ASTParser
    Changes field value specifically at its declaration, given the field name and a new initialization as String.
    changeField(Map) - Method in class ASTParser
    Changes field value specifically at its declaration, given the field name and a new initialization as String.
    changeVariable(String, String[], String, String) - Method in class ASTParser
    Given a variable name and the method it is declared in, replaces the value of the variable with a new value.
    checkRandomCreationInLoop() - Method in class ASTParser
    Determines if there are any Random variable declarations or initializations within a loop.
    close() - Method in class ASTParser
    Removes all temporary files created from loadClass().

    H

    hasManualImport() - Method in class ASTParser
    Determines if any imports are not wildcard imports (e.g.

    L

    loadClass() - Method in class ASTParser
    Converts the AST into to a runnable Java class including all changes such as modifying class constants or variables.

    T

    testFieldExists(String, String, boolean) - Method in class ASTParser
    Tests whether the program contains a field of the exact type and name, and if the field is a class constant, determines if the field has the correct modifiers.
    testMethodExists(String, String, String[]) - Method in class ASTParser
    Tests whether the program contains a method of the provided method signature, including its return type, method name, and list of parameters.
